_comment: Theme variants for the 'circuit' visual style (assets/template-circuit.html) — see themes.json's own comment for the general contract (cssVars injected as :root via __THEME_VARS__, nodePalette read by the agent during the narrative step, never injected into the template). Variable names here are circuit-specific (--s-bg, --s-panel, --s-panel-border, --s-ink, --s-ink-dim, --s-accent, --s-accent-strong, --s-solder, --s-hover-bg, --s-grid-dot) because template-circuit.html's CSS is written against this namespace, not template.html's --bg-primary/--text-primary/etc. — the two template files' CSS are never loaded together, so there is no collision, only a deliberate split so classic's themes.json is never touched by circuit changes. nodePalette hues intentionally echo the matching theme in themes.json (same brand colors), just reinterpreted here as a chip's edge-stripe/trace color instead of a full node fill.

dark-github.description: Default for the circuit style. A dark PCB-substrate palette: near-black with a green undertone, copper/amber accent, silver-grey solder/via color.

light.description: Drafting-vellum variant: pale cool paper background, deepened copper/net hues for contrast on light ground.

high-contrast.description: Presentation mode: pure black substrate, maximal ink contrast, brightened copper/net hues.
